2000.label: 🔴 Dot-Com bubble burst caused a global tech crash

2001.label: 🔴 9/11 attacks caused global panic and slowdown

2002.label: 🟡 Market stayed low due to global uncertainty

2003.label: 🟢 Market recovered strongly from the early 2000s crash

2004.label: 🟢 New government (UPA) brought fresh investor confidence

2005.label: 🟢 Strong economic growth led to a massive bull run

2006.label: 🟢 Heavy foreign investments pushed markets to new highs

2007.label: 🟢 Peak of the bull run before the global crisis

2008.label: 🔴 Global Financial Crisis (2008) caused a worldwide crash

2009.label: 🟢 Governments pumped money, causing a huge bounce back

2010.label: 🟢 Businesses fully recovered from the 2008 crash

2011.label: 🟡 European debt crisis made global investors panic

2012.label: 🟢 Market slowly stabilized after the Europe crisis

2013.label: 🟡 Indian Rupee lost value fast, causing temporary panic

2014.label: 🟢 New government (Modi) brought huge market hope

2015.label: 🟡 China's economy slowed down, dragging global markets

2017.label: 🟢 GST was launched and global markets grew together

2018.label: 🟡 IL&FS bankruptcy hurt mid-sized and small companies

2019.label: 🟢 Corporate tax was cut, making companies more profitable

2020.label: 🔴 COVID-19 lockdowns caused a severe but short crash

2021.label: 🟢 Post-COVID recovery and massive retail investments

2022.label: 🔴 Russia-Ukraine war and high inflation caused a tough market

2023.label: 🟢 Inflation cooled down, causing mid/small companies to boom

2024.label: 🟢 General elections concluded peacefully, keeping markets stable
